sheet.title: Star-Delta (Wye-Delta) Starter — 15 HP Industrial Conveyor Motor M1

items.rating: 30 A NEMA Size 1 main contactor — IEC AC-3 equivalent, coil 120 VAC
K1 NO aux hold contact (mechanically linked)
30 A NEMA Size 1 STAR (wye) contactor — closes during start, shorts motor windings into wye
30 A NEMA Size 1 DELTA contactor — closes after transition, reconnects windings for run
K2 NC aux electrical interlock — opens when K2 picks, blocks K3 coil
K3 NC aux electrical interlock — opens when K3 picks, blocks K2 coil (cross-interlock)
Class 10 thermal — wired in motor-phase circuit, set 21 A motor FLA
15 HP / 460 V / 21 A FLA / 3-phase induction motor (6-lead delta-wound for star-delta starting)
Transition timer 7 s on-delay (star → delta transition)
STOP — red mushroom actuator, NC 21-22 in coil-break path
Incoming power terminal block L1/L2/L3, 6 AWG / 13 mm² DIN-rail
Motor outgoing terminal block U1/V1/W1 + U2/V2/W2 (six-lead motor) + PE, 10 AWG / 5.3 mm²
Control supply L/N 120 VAC, 14 AWG / 2 mm² DIN-rail

connections.function: Incoming L1 to disconnect switch pole 1
Incoming L2 to Q1 pole 2
Incoming L3 to Q1 pole 3
Disconnected L1 to MAIN contactor K1 pole 1
Q1 to K1 pole 2
Q1 to K1 pole 3
K1 output L1 to overload F1 phase L1
K1 L2 to F1 phase L2
K1 L3 to F1 phase L3
F1 output L1 → motor lead U1 (winding 1 start end)
F1 output L2 → motor lead V1
F1 output L3 → motor lead W1
STAR contactor K2 output → motor lead U2 (winding 1 finish end) — K2 closes wye-point during start
K2 → motor lead V2
K2 → motor lead W2 (forms wye point at K2 common when K2 closes)
K2 internal star-point bond pole 1 to pole 2 (wye-point common)
K2 internal star-point bond pole 2 to pole 3
DELTA contactor K3 output → motor lead U2 (parallel with K2 lead U2)
K3 → motor lead V2
K3 → motor lead W2
K1 L1 cross-tied to K3 input pole 1 (delta closure: L1→U2 path)
K1 L2 cross-tied to K3 input pole 2 (delta closure: L2→V2)
K1 L3 cross-tied to K3 input pole 3 (delta closure: L3→W2)
Motor lead U1 to motor terminal U1
Motor lead V1 to motor terminal V1
Motor lead W1 to motor terminal W1
Motor lead U2 to motor terminal U2
Motor lead V2 to motor terminal V2
Motor lead W2 to motor terminal W2
Control L 120 VAC to F1 trip NC 95
F1 96 to STOP NC 21
STOP 22 to START NO 13
START 14 to K1 main coil A1
K1 coil A2 to N
STOP 22 to K1-AUX 13 (hold-in branch)
K1-AUX 14 holds K1 coil after S1 release
K1 main proven → K3 cross-interlock NC 21 (K3 not picked, so closed)
K3-NC-K2 22 to STAR contactor K2 coil A1 (energise wye)
K2 coil return to N
K1 main proven → KT1 7s transition timer energised
KT1 coil return to N
KT1 timed-out NC opens K2 coil-break path → K2 drops out at t = 7 s
KT1 timed-out NO → K2-NC-K3 cross-interlock NC 21 (K2 dropped, so K2-NC-K3 has closed back NC)
K2-NC-K3 22 to DELTA contactor K3 coil A1 — energise delta
K3 coil return to N
K2 NO aux 13-14 drives H2 STARTING amber lamp
H2 return to N
K3 NO aux 13-14 drives H1 RUN green lamp
H1 return to N

labels.text: K1/K2/K3 use IEEE Std 315 §22.1.1 contactor symbol (US convention) alongside BS EN 60617 IEC 617-7
K2 (star) and K3 (delta) electrically cross-interlocked AND mechanically interlocked — cannot close simultaneously
Open-transition star-delta: K2 drops fully before K3 picks, brief dead-time on motor — acceptable for conveyor load, not for high-inertia loads

sequence_of_operation: START STAR PHASE (t = 0): Operator presses S1 → F1 95-96 NC closed + S2 21-22 NC closed + S1 13-14 closes → K1 main coil energises → K1 main poles close (3-phase reaches F1 then motor U1/V1/W1 leads). Simultaneously K1-AUX 13-14 closes providing hold-in + drives two parallel control paths: (a) energises K2 STAR contactor coil via K3-NC-K2 cross-interlock (K3 not yet picked, so K3-NC-K2 path is closed) — K2 closes and shorts motor leads U2/V2/W2 into a common wye-point, motor windings now configured in wye (each winding sees 460/√3 = 266 V phase voltage); (b) energises KT1 7 s on-delay timer. Motor accelerates in wye configuration drawing reduced starting current (~33% of DOL inrush) for 7 s. K2 NO aux 13-14 drives H2 STARTING amber lamp. TRANSITION (t = 7 s): KT1 times out → KT1 15 NC opens K2 coil-break path → K2 drops out (motor disconnected from wye-point common; motor now coasts briefly with windings open at U2/V2/W2 end). Critical: K2-NC-K3 cross-interlock contact has been open during the wye phase (K2 was picked) — when K2 drops, K2-NC-K3 closes NC, and the KT1 timed-out NO contact 18 simultaneously energises K3 DELTA contactor coil via the now-closed K2-NC-K3 path. K3 picks → K3 main poles close → motor leads U2/V2/W2 reconnected to L2/L3/L1 (cross-tied delta) → motor windings now in delta (each winding sees full 460 V line voltage). K3 NO aux 13-14 drives H1 RUN green lamp; H2 STARTING extinguishes. RUN STEADY-STATE: Motor in delta configuration draws full 21 A FLA at rated torque. K1 + K3 + F1 in series carry motor current; K2 dropped and isolated. STOP: Operator presses S2 → S2 21-22 opens → K1 + KT1 + K3 (and K2 if still picked) all drop together → motor coasts. TRIP: F1 thermal overload (sustained > 24 A) opens 95-96 → identical drop-out as STOP.

assumptions: NEC 2023 Article 430 covers motors, motor circuits, and controllers — including reduced-voltage starting (e.g. star-delta / wye-delta) which is explicitly identified as an alternative to DOL for inrush reduction.

Article 430 Part IV (motor branch-circuit short-circuit and ground-fault protection) — upstream 30 A inverse-time breaker complies with Article 430 Table requirements for a 21 A FLA 460 V motor at 250% FLA = 52.5 A maximum, 30 A is conservative below that ceiling.

IEEE Std 315 is the US symbol cross-reference standard — schematic uses both BS EN 60617 (primary) and IEEE Std 315 (cross-reference in ieee_std_315_ref where applicable) per US documentation practice.
UL 508A applies to industrial control panel construction where this schematic is assembled — covers SCCR (short-circuit current rating), enclosure rating, marking, and terminal-block torque values.

Open-transition star-delta is appropriate for a conveyor (low-to-moderate inertia, no critical dead-time constraint); for a high-inertia load (centrifugal compressor, fan with large flywheel) a closed-transition with transition resistor would be required to avoid the brief open-circuit during K2 drop / K3 pick.
Upstream rating consumed from db-layout-rollup (panelboard PNL-A 30 A circuit) — recorded in meta.consumed_intents[].
No fault-level intent consumed; the prospective fault current at PNL-A engineer-declared via inputs.json.
No earthing intent consumed; US grounding (separately-derived TN-S equivalent — service entrance bonding per NEC 2023 Article 250) engineer-declared via inputs.json.

rationale.chat_summary: Open-transition star-delta (wye-delta) starter for 15 HP / 460 V / 21 A FLA industrial conveyor motor M1, US jurisdiction (NEC 2023). K1 main + K2 star + K3 delta NEMA Size 1 contactors with mechanical + electrical cross-interlock; KT1 7 s on-delay transition. K1-AUX hold; F1 Class 10 overload; 2 indicator lamps. Consumes db-layout-rollup (PNL-A 30 A). Reply to refine (e.g. add closed-transition resistor for high-inertia load).

sections.title: Schematic Identification
sections.summary: control_motor_starter (star-delta / wye-delta sub-topology) on sheet SHT-004 for a US industrial conveyor application. Subject is M1, a 15 HP / 460 V / 21 A FLA 6-lead delta-wound 3-phase induction motor driving an industrial conveyor. Open-transition star-delta is the reduced-voltage starting method chosen — it cuts starting current to ~33% of DOL inrush by configuring the motor in wye (winding-line voltage = Vline/√3) during the start interval, then reconnecting to delta (winding-line voltage = Vline) for full-voltage steady-state running. The schematic uses both BS EN 60617 IEC 617-7 (primary symbol system) and IEEE Std 315 (US cross-reference, populated in ieee_std_315_ref fields). One intent consumed: db-layout-rollup → PNL-A 30 A circuit.

decisions.label: Open-transition star-delta for 15 HP conveyor
decisions.summary: Conveyor is low-to-moderate inertia — brief open-transition dead-time (~30-50 ms) is acceptable; closed-transition resistor unnecessary.
decisions.rule: Star-delta sub-topology selection by load inertia
decisions.code_clause: NEC 2023 Article 430 (motors, motor circuits, and controllers — reduced-voltage starting)

decisions.label: Dual-symbol convention BS EN 60617 + IEEE Std 315
decisions.summary: US industrial schematics commonly cross-reference both BS EN 60617 (international) and IEEE Std 315 (US) — ieee_std_315_ref populated where applicable.

decisions.label: Upstream rating consumed from db-layout-rollup
decisions.summary: PNL-A 30 A circuit rating from db-layout-rollup drives Q1/K1/K2/K3 selection.

sections.title: Source Context
sections.summary: Upstream: PNL-A 30 A 3-phase circuit, 460 V 3-phase + N supply, US-style separately-derived (TN-S equivalent) grounding with service-entrance N-G bond per NEC 2023 Article 250. db-layout-rollup intent supplied the 30 A circuit rating. Motor FLA 21 A × 250% = 52.5 A maximum branch-circuit short-circuit protection per NEC 2023 Article 430 Part IV — the engineer-declared 30 A inverse-time breaker is conservative below the 52.5 A ceiling and matches the available frame size on a NEMA Size 1 starter. No fault-level intent consumed — prospective fault current engineer-declared in inputs.json; no earthing intent — US grounding strategy engineer-declared.

decisions.label: 30 A upstream below Article 430 Part IV 250% ceiling
decisions.summary: 21 A FLA × 250% = 52.5 A ceiling; 30 A engineer-declared sits well below for headroom + matches NEMA Size 1 frame.
decisions.code_clause: NEC 2023 Article 430 (motor branch-circuit short-circuit and ground-fault protection)

decisions.label: Two-intent absence disclosed
decisions.summary: fault-level + earthing not consumed; values engineer-declared and re-stated in compliance_summary.assumptions[].
decisions.rule: Partial-intent disclosure per generator prompt Step 11

sections.title: Items Breakdown
sections.summary: 17 devices placed. Power: Q1 (30 A 3-pole disconnect), K1 (NEMA Size 1 main contactor) + K1-AUX (hold), K2 (NEMA Size 1 star contactor — closes wye-point at U2/V2/W2), K3 (NEMA Size 1 delta contactor — reconnects U2/V2/W2 to cross-tied L2/L3/L1 for delta-mode), K2-NC-K3 + K3-NC-K2 (cross-interlock NC auxiliaries), F1 (Class 10 thermal set 21 A), M1 (15 HP 6-lead delta-wound motor). Control: KT1 (7 s on-delay transition timer), S1 START, S2 STOP. Indication: H1 green (RUN / delta) + H2 amber (STARTING / star). 3 terminal rails X1/X2/X3 segregate incoming power, six-lead motor outgoing power (12 motor leads total including PE), and 120 VAC control supply.

decisions.label: NEMA Size 1 contactors for 15 HP motor at 460 V
decisions.summary: NEMA Size 1 is rated 27 A and 7.5-15 HP at 460 V — exactly fits this motor; corresponds approximately to IEC AC-3 30 A rating used elsewhere.

decisions.label: K2/K3 cross-interlock (electrical + mechanical)
decisions.summary: K2 NC-aux blocks K3 coil while K2 is picked; K3 NC-aux blocks K2 coil while K3 is picked; mechanical interlock between K2 and K3 backs up the electrical interlock — physically impossible to close both simultaneously.

decisions.label: F1 Class 10 set 21 A FLA
decisions.summary: Star starting reduces motor current ~3× during the 7 s star phase — Class 10 envelope (10 s at 7.2×Ie) easily covers the reduced-current start; 21 A setpoint matches motor FLA at full delta-mode running.

sections.title: Connection Topology
sections.summary: Power (W1-W29, power_LV, 6 AWG line / 10 AWG motor cable): X1 → Q1 → K1 → F1 → X2 U1/V1/W1 → M1 winding-start ends. Star phase: K2 inputs paralleled via internal star-point bond (W16-W17) closes the wye common at motor U2/V2/W2 ends. Delta phase: K3 inputs cross-tied to K1 outputs (W21-W23: K1-L1 → K3-1, K1-L2 → K3-2, K1-L3 → K3-3) such that K3 output reconnects U2/V2/W2 to L2/L3/L1 (delta closure). Control (W30-W45, control_LV, 14 AWG 120 VAC): canonical S2-NC → S1-NO → K1 coil with K1-AUX hold; K1 main proven drives both K2 coil (via K3-NC-K2 cross-interlock) and KT1 timer. After 7 s KT1 NC opens K2, KT1 NO + K2-NC-K3 cross-interlock energises K3. Indication (W46-W49, aux_AC): K2 NO aux → H2 STARTING amber, K3 NO aux → H1 RUN green.

decisions.label: K3 inputs cross-tied to K1 line phases for delta closure
decisions.summary: W21-W23 deliberately tie K3-1/3/5 to K1-L1/L2/L3 so K3 output to motor U2/V2/W2 wires the delta: L1-U1-W2-L3 + L2-V1-U2-L1 + L3-W1-V2-L2.

decisions.label: Open-transition: K2 drops fully before K3 picks
decisions.summary: KT1 NC opens K2 then KT1 NO + K2-NC-K3 path picks K3 — mechanical drop-out time ~30-50 ms = brief motor coast; acceptable for conveyor inertia.
decisions.rule: Open-transition sequence (vs closed-transition with bridging resistor)

sections.title: Compliance Assessment
sections.summary: Compliant against NEC 2023 Article 430 (motor branch-circuit + overload + reduced-voltage starting), NEC 2023 Article 250 (grounding and bonding — service entrance N-G bond declared), UL 508A (industrial control panel construction + SCCR), IEEE Std 315 (US symbol cross-reference), and BS EN 60617 IEC 617-7 (primary symbol system, jurisdiction-agnostic). NEMA ICS 2 governs contactor sizing. No CRITICAL or HIGH flags. Eight assumptions documented including the open-transition vs closed-transition load-inertia caveat and the two-intent partial absences.

decisions.label: US-jurisdiction primary citation form
decisions.summary: NEC Article-level references throughout; no BS 7671 or KS 1700 appears. Article 430 referenced at the Article level rather than sub-clause where the precise sub-clause cannot be cross-verified — per generator prompt under-cite policy.

decisions.label: Dual-standard symbol convention
decisions.summary: BS EN 60617 IEC 617-7 (primary) + IEEE Std 315 (US cross-reference) — standard US industrial-schematic convention.

sections.title: Schedule Notes
sections.summary: Hybrid-mode: db-layout-rollup consumed (PNL-A 30 A); fault-level + earthing absent. Open-transition was selected because the conveyor is low-to-moderate inertia and the 30-50 ms K2-drop / K3-pick dead-time is acceptable. For a high-inertia load (75 HP centrifugal compressor or large flywheel) the dead-time causes inrush on K3 close as the motor decelerates during the dead window — closed-transition with a bridging resistor + 4th contactor K4 is required. Motor wiring requires 6 separately-terminated leads (U1/V1/W1 + U2/V2/W2) — not 3-lead delta. A 9-lead dual-voltage motor or 3-lead delta breaks the starter: motor PO must call out 6-lead delta-wound construction.

decisions.label: Hybrid-mode: 1 intent consumed, 2 absent
decisions.rule: Hybrid-mode disclosure per generator prompt Step 11

decisions.label: 6-lead delta-wound motor required
decisions.summary: Motor MUST be 6-lead delta-wound — purchase order specification critical.
